item.title: FirstFT: SpaceX investors asked to buy Musk’s ‘moonshots’
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Hungary’s anti-graft AI and GSK-Nuvalent merger talks

item.title: Africa’s Fastest-Growing Companies
item.description: South Africa dominates the 2026 rankings as Kenya leapfrogs Nigeria into second place and an Egyptian business tops the list for the first time. Plus: fintechs offer a fix to Africa’s financial problems and a solar success with more than 50mn customers across the continent

item.title: Inside the battle to break up the world’s oldest bank
item.description: Monte dei Paschi emerged from public ownership to become an improbable consolidator. Intesa and BPM have it in their sights

item.title: Italy’s World Cup of banking drama
item.description: Rival moves for Monte dei Paschi are setting the stage for a rare takeover battle in Italian banking

item.title: Trump critic Doug Ford urges president to ‘get a deal’ with Canada
item.description: Pugnacious Ontario premier who has sparred with the president takes diplomatic tone on Washington visit

item.title: Why are we still arguing about the industrial revolution?
item.description: Historical data does not offer much insight about how our predecessors navigated profound change

item.title: There is a simpler option for making AI pay its way: tax it properly
item.description: The world of laissez-faire no longer exists given the impact of the technology

item.title: UK turns to imported chicken as demand surges
item.description: Producers blame restrictive planning rules and more stringent welfare regulations for limiting domestic expansion

item.title: SpaceX’s $1.78tn IPO asks investors to buy Musk’s moonshots
item.description: Public markets asked to price in advances in AI, Starlink and space-based computing long before lift-off

item.title: US and Japanese banks boost fossil fuel finance, campaign report says
item.description: JPMorgan dominant with 12% increase in deals while European banks reduced market share

item.title: New Covent Garden’s fruit and veg have gone upmarket
item.description: The historic trading place now caters to Michelin-starred restaurants, hotels and grocery stores

item.title: BP investors push for clarity over ousting of chair
item.description: Lack of detail on why Albert Manifold was fired sows doubts over culture overhaul

item.title: Renault to cap defence revenue at 5% as it pushes into drone making
item.description: French carmaker’s cautious approach partly motivated by desire to stick to ESG guidelines

item.title: ASML chief warns EU against directing chip supplies
item.description: Industry needs ‘champions’, not intervention, says Christophe Fouquet, head of Europe’s biggest listed company

item.title: Earnings, not valuations, are fuelling the US stock market
item.description: Gains are spurred by the strength of profits rather than rising valuations or speculative fervour

item.title: ITV set for World Cup ad record as AI groups pile in
item.description: Spending by US tech companies and major expansion of 2026 tournament lift revenues at struggling UK broadcaster

item.title: AI used to hunt Viktor Orbán’s alleged corruption
item.description: Head of Hungary’s anti-graft body says €160bn may have been siphoned off during ex-PM’s 16 years in power

item.title: Nigeria’s big food businesses prosper through insecurity and economic shock
item.description: BUA Foods boss says company defied cost-of-living crisis because ‘everybody must eat’

item.title: ‘Age of electricity’ lights the path for Sun King’s solar success
item.description: Company’s off-grid energy infrastructure is used by more than 50mn customers across sub-Saharan Africa

item.title: ‘Picks and shovels’ companies profit from data centre scramble
item.description: Caterpillar and Hochtief among the once-staid industrial stocks lifted by the AI boom

item.title: Kenya Airways: riding out storm after storm
item.description: CEO of national carrier sanguine about future despite impact of Middle East war just after recovery from Covid pandemic

item.title: Trading app drives change in Egypt’s investment culture
item.description: Thndr co-founder says 2023 financial crisis a pivotal moment for retail investor customer base that has grown beyond his ‘wildest dreams’

item.title: Africa’s manufacturers struggle against the odds
item.description: Productivity-pay gap, expensive power and poor infrastructure act as a drag on the sector

item.title: Fintechs offer fix to Africa’s financial problems
item.description: Digital platforms have revolutionised lives of millions as they bypass creaking banking systems across the continent

item.title: Pentagon restores Alibaba, Baidu and BYD to Chinese military groups blacklist
item.description: Three companies reinstated as US national security risk after sudden removal in February

item.title: GSK in talks to buy cancer biotech Nuvalent for more than $9bn
item.description: Deal would rank as one of UK drugmaker’s biggest as biotech M&A activity accelerates this year

item.title: Has India lost the AI race? Not entirely
item.description: Also in this newsletter, what’s going on with Rajesh Exports?

item.title: Apple unveils ‘Siri AI’ in challenge to rival chatbots
item.description: Silicon Valley giant promises user privacy as it makes long-delayed overhaul of voice assistant

item.title: FT Financial Literacy and Inclusion Campaign
item.description: The FT invites readers to join our campaign to promote financial literacy in the UK and around the world

item.title: Apollo and Blackstone raise $35bn in chip financing deal for Anthropic
item.description: Transaction is one of the largest private credit fundraisings, fuelling the Claude maker’s AI growth plans
